text:
Centre for Epilepsy

Neurosciences Division

Clinic Date: 06 January 2021

Dr Amelia Hart
Riverside Medical Practice
48 Mill Lane, Oxford OX1 2AB

Dear Dr Amelia Hart,

Daniel Turner, DOB: 03-11-1989, Hospital No: H632914 NHS No. 9842156730
22 Meadow Close, Abingdon, OX14 1QP

Subject: Correspondence recipients for ongoing epilepsy communications

Summary: I reviewed Daniel Turner by telephone today regarding work-related factors. They undertake gardening and groundskeeping duties outdoors, with exposure to heat and prolonged exertion that varies seasonally. We agreed to streamline future correspondence so updates reach all relevant parties promptly, particularly during periods of increased outdoor workload.

Please send all future epilepsy-related communications (clinic letters, medication updates, blood test results, occupational advice) to the following recipients:

Primary recipient:
- GP: Dr Amelia Hart, Riverside Medical Practice, 48 Mill Lane, Oxford OX1 2AB

Copy to:
- Patient: Daniel Turner, 22 Meadow Close, Abingdon, OX14 1QP
- Occupational Health (Employer): GreenScape Grounds Maintenance OH Dept, Unit 5, Hawthorn Industrial Park, Didcot OX11 7RQ
- Community Pharmacist: Lime Tree Pharmacy, 6 High Street, Abingdon OX14 5AX
- Epilepsy Specialist Nurse Team: Thames Valley Epilepsy Specialist Nurse Team, Neurosciences, Churchill Hospital, Old Road, Oxford OX3 7LE

Rationale: Ensures timely sharing of advice relevant to outdoor work patterns, heat exposure, hydration guidance, and medication supply planning during peak seasonal duties.

Please update practice records accordingly. I will maintain this distribution list unless notified of changes by the patient or GP.

Yours sincerely,

check__Seizure Frequency Number.analysis: The letter focuses on updating correspondence recipients and work-related considerations. It contains no statements about seizures occurring, their frequency, or seizure freedom duration. There are no numeric or qualitative frequency descriptors (e.g., per week/month, seizure-free for X months). Therefore, according to the rules, since seizures are not mentioned at all, the correct label is 'no seizure frequency reference'.
reference: Create a reasonable NHS letter summarising correspondence recipients (copy-to list) for epilepsy communications

flaws.description: No seizure frequency referenced in gold
Row marked as not OK in raw dataset
